message:
feat(installer): add JSON summary emission (bd-31ps.3.2)

Add acfs_summary_emit() that produces a stable JSON summary of each
install run at ~/.acfs/logs/install_summary_<timestamp>.json. The
summary reads phase data from state.json (completed_phases,
phase_durations, failure info) and adds environment metadata
(ACFS version, mode, Ubuntu version, target user) plus a pointer
to the log file from bd-31ps.3.1.

Emitted on both success (with total_seconds) and failure (in
cleanup trap). Schema version 1 for downstream tooling stability.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-21kh","title":"Progress bar during tool installation","description":"## Overview
Add a visual progress bar during tool installation showing overall progress and current tool being installed.

## Current Problem
- Installation can take several minutes
- Users see scrolling text but no sense of overall progress
- No way to estimate time remaining
- Anxiety: "Is it stuck or still working?"

## Proposed UI
```
Installing ACFS tools...
[████████████░░░░░░░░] 12/26 tools (46%)
Current: Installing rust toolchain...
```

## Implementation Details
1. Count total tools to install upfront
2. Update progress after each tool completes
3. Show current tool name during install
4. Use ANSI escape codes for in-place updates
5. Respect NO_COLOR environment variable

## Progress Calculation
- Parse manifest.yaml for tool count
- Subtract already-installed tools
- Increment counter after each install_tool() call

## Terminal Handling
```bash
# In-place update (same line)
printf '\r[%-20s] %d/%d tools (%d%%)' "$bar" "$done" "$total" "$pct"

# With NO_COLOR or non-TTY
echo "[$done/$total] Installing $tool..."
```

## Graceful Degradation
- Non-interactive: Simple line-by-line output
- NO_COLOR: Text-only progress
- Narrow terminal: Abbreviated format

## Test Plan
- [ ] Test progress updates correctly
- [ ] Test NO_COLOR fallback
- [ ] Test non-TTY output (piped)
- [ ] Test narrow terminal handling
- [ ] Visual test: progress matches actual completion

## Files to Modify
- scripts/lib/install_helpers.sh (add progress tracking)
- scripts/install.sh (initialize progress bar)
- New: scripts/lib/progress.sh (progress bar utilities)","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:01:45.195454182Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T23:07:22.822272754Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-21kh","depends_on_id":"bd-3y1n","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:03:54.194943376Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-22gc","title":"Deep exploration: SLB (Simultaneous Launch Button)","description":"## Goal
Perform deep exploration of SLB (Simultaneous Launch Button) and revise its description on the flywheel/TLDR pages with comprehensive testing.

### 0.1 Tool Existence Check
```bash
# Verify SLB installation
[[ -d /dp/simultaneous_launch_button ]] && echo "PASS: slb repo exists" || { echo "FAIL: slb repo missing"; exit 1; }
command -v slb &>/dev/null && echo "PASS: slb command available" || { echo "FAIL: slb not in PATH"; exit 1; }
```

```bash
SNAPSHOT_DIR=/tmp/slb-exploration-snapshots-$(date +%Y%m%d-%H%M%S)
```

Content must match FlywheelTool interface:

- features[], cliCommands[], connectsTo[]

- Check for two-person rule docs, coordination protocol

### 1.4 External Context Search

### 2.1 Core Capabilities Verification
Document findings for each area:
- [ ] Two-person rule mechanism
- [ ] Confirmation protocol and timeout
- [ ] Command types requiring two-person approval
- [ ] Integration with dcg (destructive command guard)
- [ ] Recovery from failed confirmations

### 2.2 Synergy Verification
Cross-reference these tools actually integrate:
- [ ] dcg - destructive command protection
- [ ] mail - confirmation messaging
- [ ] ntm - multi-agent coordination

### 2.3 Safety Verification
```bash
# Check what commands trigger two-person rule
cat /dp/simultaneous_launch_button/config/*.yaml 2>/dev/null | head -20
# Or check code for command patterns
grep -r "dangerous\|destructive" /dp/simultaneous_launch_button/src/ 2>/dev/null | head -10
```

## Phase 3: Revision (UPDATE DESCRIPTIONS)

### 3.1 Update apps/web/lib/flywheel.ts
Update slb entry with VERIFIED information:
- `tagline`: Two-person rule for dangerous commands
- `description`: Coordination protocol
- `deepDescription`: How confirmation works
- `features`: Verified safety capabilities
- `cliCommands`: Only commands that actually work
- `connectsTo`: Only verified integrations (dcg, mail, ntm)

### 3.2 Update apps/web/lib/tldr-content.ts
Update TldrFlywheelTool entry with:
- `briefDescription`: Technical summary
- `bulletPoints`: Verified capabilities
- `synergyExamples`: Working integration examples

### 4.1 TypeScript Compilation
```bash
cd apps/web && npx tsc --noEmit 2>&1 | head -20
```

### 4.2 Unit Tests
```bash
# Test slb entry structure
node -e "
const { flywheelTools } = require('./lib/flywheel');
const slb = flywheelTools.find(t => t.id === 'slb');
console.log('Testing slb entry...');
console.assert(slb, 'slb entry exists');
console.assert(slb.features?.length > 0, 'has features');
```

### 4.3 E2E Test: Coordination Flow (Safe Mode)
```bash
#\!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail
LOG=/tmp/slb-e2e-$(date +%Y%m%d-%H%M%S).log

echo "=== SLB E2E Test ===" | tee $LOG
echo "Started: $(date)" | tee -a $LOG

# Test 1: Status check
echo "Test 1: Status check..." | tee -a $LOG
slb status 2>&1 | tee -a $LOG

# Test 2: Help for confirm
echo "Test 2: Confirm help..." | tee -a $LOG
slb confirm --help 2>&1 | head -5 | tee -a $LOG

# Test 3: List pending (if available)
echo "Test 3: Pending operations..." | tee -a $LOG
slb pending 2>&1 | tee -a $LOG || echo "No pending command"

echo "=== All Tests Passed ===" | tee -a $LOG
```

### 4.4 Content Diff Verification
```bash
echo "=== Changes Made ==="
diff $SNAPSHOT_DIR/flywheel.ts.before apps/web/lib/flywheel.ts || true
```

### 5.1 Rollback Procedure (if tests fail)

```bash
cp $SNAPSHOT_DIR/tldr-content.ts.before apps/web/lib/tldr-content.ts
echo "Rolled back to pre-exploration state"
```

### 5.2 Sync Changes
```bash
br update bd-22gc --status done
br sync --flush-only
```

### 5.3 Final Verification

-{"id":"bd-29fl","title":"acfs status: One-line health summary command","description":"## Overview
Add `acfs status` command that outputs a single-line health summary, perfect for shell prompts or quick checks.

## Current Problem
- `acfs doctor` is verbose and takes time to run
- No quick way to check "is everything OK?"
- Shell prompt integration requires parseable output

## Proposed Output Format
```bash
$ acfs status
ACFS OK: 26 tools, last update 2h ago

$ acfs status  # with issues
ACFS WARN: 2 outdated tools, doctor recommended
```

## Exit Codes
- 0: Everything healthy
- 1: Warnings (outdated tools, minor issues)
- 2: Errors (missing tools, broken state)

## Implementation Details
1. New file: `scripts/commands/status.sh`
2. Quick checks only (no network calls by default):
   - State file exists and valid
   - Required tools present in PATH
   - Last update timestamp
3. Optional `--check-updates` for network-based checks

## Machine-Readable Mode
```bash
$ acfs status --json
{"status":"ok","tools":26,"last_update":"2026-01-25T21:00:00Z","warnings":[]}
```

## Shell Prompt Integration
```bash
# Add to .bashrc
acfs_status() { acfs status --short 2>/dev/null || echo "?"; }
PS1='$(acfs_status) \w $ '
```

## Test Plan
- [ ] Test exit codes for all states
- [ ] Test --json output is valid JSON
- [ ] Test runs in < 100ms (no network)
- [ ] Test with missing state file
- [ ] Test with outdated state

## Files to Create

+# ============================================================
+# Install summary JSON (bd-31ps.3.2)
+# ============================================================
+
+# Emit a JSON summary of the install run for downstream tooling.
+# Usage: acfs_summary_emit <status> [total_seconds]
+#   status: "success" or "failure"
+#   total_seconds: total wall-clock time (optional, default 0)
+# Output: ~/.acfs/logs/install_summary_<timestamp>.json
+acfs_summary_emit() {
+    local status="$1"
+    local total_seconds="${2:-0}"
+
+    # Require jq (installed by ensure_base_deps before phases run)
+    command -v jq &>/dev/null || return 1
+
+    local summary_dir="${ACFS_HOME:-/home/${TARGET_USER:?}/.acfs}/logs"
+    mkdir -p "$summary_dir" 2>/dev/null || return 1
+
+    ACFS_SUMMARY_FILE="${summary_dir}/install_summary_$(date +%Y%m%d_%H%M%S).json"
+    export ACFS_SUMMARY_FILE
+
+    # Read phase data from state.json if available
+    local phases_json="[]"
+    local failure_json="null"
+    if [[ -f "${ACFS_STATE_FILE:-}" ]] && command -v jq &>/dev/null; then
+        # Build phases array: [{id, name, duration_seconds}] in completion order
+        phases_json=$(jq -r '
+            (.completed_phases // []) as $completed |
+            (.phase_durations // {}) as $durations |
+            [$completed[] | {id: ., duration_seconds: ($durations[.] // null)}]
+        ' "$ACFS_STATE_FILE" 2>/dev/null) || phases_json="[]"
+
+        # Build failure object if present
+        failure_json=$(jq -r '
+            if .failed_phase != null then
+                {phase: .failed_phase, step: .failed_step, error: .failed_error, resume_hint: "--resume"}
+            else null end
+        ' "$ACFS_STATE_FILE" 2>/dev/null) || failure_json="null"
+    fi
+
+    # Get Ubuntu version
+    local ubuntu_version="unknown"
+    if command -v lsb_release &>/dev/null; then
+        ubuntu_version=$(lsb_release -rs 2>/dev/null) || ubuntu_version="unknown"
+    fi
+
+    # Construct the summary JSON
+    jq -n \
+        --argjson schema_version 1 \
+        --arg status "$status" \
+        --arg timestamp "$(date -Iseconds)" \
+        --argjson total_seconds "$total_seconds" \
+        --arg acfs_version "${ACFS_VERSION:-unknown}" \
+        --arg mode "${MODE:-unknown}" \
+        --arg ubuntu_version "$ubuntu_version" \
+        --arg target_user "${TARGET_USER:-unknown}" \
+        --arg target_home "${TARGET_HOME:-unknown}" \
+        --argjson phases "$phases_json" \
+        --argjson failure "$failure_json" \
+        --arg log_file "${ACFS_LOG_FILE:-}" \
+        '{
+            schema_version: $schema_version,
+            status: $status,
+            timestamp: $timestamp,
+            total_seconds: $total_seconds,
+            environment: {
+                acfs_version: $acfs_version,
+                mode: $mode,
+                ubuntu_version: $ubuntu_version,
+                target_user: $target_user,
+                target_home: $target_home
+            },
+            phases: $phases,
+            failure: $failure,
+            log_file: (if $log_file != "" then $log_file else null end)
+        }' > "$ACFS_SUMMARY_FILE" 2>/dev/null || return 1
+
+    # Fix ownership so target user can read
+    if [[ -n "${TARGET_USER:-}" ]] && [[ "$(id -u)" -eq 0 ]]; then
+        chown "${TARGET_USER}:${TARGET_USER}" "$ACFS_SUMMARY_FILE" 2>/dev/null || true
+    fi
+
+    log_detail "Summary: $ACFS_SUMMARY_FILE"
+}
+
